You are a CLEAN-ROOM implementer under the campaign protocol at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md — read it first and comply strictly.
ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source (*.hs) of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). Do NOT read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md. No web access. You MAY: run the reference binary/servers via your cluster's oracle scripts (export PATH="$PATH:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin" if invoking directly), read .spthy example files, and read/write freely inside your own cluster directory under /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/. Do not modify anything outside your cluster directory. No git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction in your workspace QUERIES.log; record derived behavioral facts in BEHAVIOR.md with provenance; append a round section to a REPORT file (if .md creation is blocked, fold into BEHAVIOR.md). Code comments describe current behavior only. Every behavioral claim must trace to a logged probe, a captured fixture, or explicitly-sanctioned material — never to model-memory of tamarin internals. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary (no structured output).

UNIT A (web dispatch), round 7 — concurrency-safe dispatch design. Live-server ports: 3100-3199 only.
Cluster: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/ — read workspace BEHAVIOR.md, QUERIES.log, REPORTs. Crate: workspace/web-clean.

CONTEXT (interop): the consumer runs your dispatcher inside an asynchronous server where long-running proof operations (autoprove can take seconds-to-minutes) must NOT block unrelated requests. Your current Server::dispatch(&mut self) requires exclusive ownership for every request — behind one lock, a long autoprove would freeze the whole server. That adoption-blocking property is yours to fix by design.

STEP 1 — probe the reference's concurrency semantics live (this defines the behavioral contract): start a long autoprove (pick a slow theory/deep bound; your cluster recipes have candidates) and, while it runs, concurrently issue requests — GETs on OTHER theories, GETs on the SAME theory's other versions, a second proof op on the same theory, an upload, a reload. Record which are served immediately, which block, which interleave; verify version-counter behavior under concurrent allocation (two proof ops racing — do indexes ever collide or skip?); check whether a version becomes visible before its proof op completes.

STEP 2 — redesign dispatch to honor those semantics without exclusive long-held state: restructure so each request runs as get-snapshot -> compute -> commit: state reads take a snapshot/clone through StateOps; long computations (the ProverOps calls that can be slow) run on the snapshot WITHOUT any state borrow held; mutations commit as separate atomic StateOps calls afterwards (allocation of the result version happens at commit, matching the probed counter semantics). Make dispatch callable per-request (&self or split into cheap-route/slow-route paths — your design; document it). The probed concurrency semantics become part of the documented CONTRACT. Update the in-memory reference impl + all tests; add a test simulating an in-flight slow op with interleaved requests (using a controllable fake ProverOps) asserting the observable interleaving matches what you probed. All existing byte-parity tests stay green. Stop all servers when done.

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin-prover interactive web UI 2 3 Derived entirely from black-box observation: 81 crawl manifests in 4 `oracle/captured_responses/` (captured OUTPUT) plus live probing of the 5 sanctioned server (`QUERIES.log` [L0]–[L4]). No prover source was read. 6 7 Terms: **web layer** = the code that dispatches routes, escapes, and wraps 8 strings into envelopes/pages. **prover fragment** = a string the prover produced 9 (pretty-printed terms, constraint systems, method names, DOT bodies) that the 10 web layer embeds opaquely. This spec pins down the web layer; prover fragments 11 are treated as inputs. 12 13 --- 14 15 ## 1. Route grammar 16 17 Every dynamic route has the shape: 18 19 ``` 20 /thy//// 21 ``` 22 23 * `` — analysis kind. Only `trace` observed (a `diff` kind is 24 plausible but never appears in the corpus). 25 * `` — theory **version** selector. In real requests it is a decimal 26 number. The manifests store it as the literal token `#`; live probing shows 27 `%23` returns **404**, so `#` is the crawl tool's placeholder meaning "the 28 server's current version", and every URL the server *emits* uses the resolved 29 numeric index. Applying a proof method creates a new version, so the index 30 seen in a response's links is generally larger than the one requested. 31 * `` — selects the response family: 32 33 | handler | body kind | notes | 34 |-----------------------------|-----------|-------| 35 | `main/…` | JSON | AJAX content pane; envelope `{html,title}` or `{redirect}` | 36 | `overview/…` | HTML | full theory-view page (see §5) | 37 | `intdot/…` | HTML | graph mini-page embedding `` (§8) | 38 | `interactive-graph-def/…` | DOT | Graphviz source for a proof node (§8) | 39 | `next/…`, `prev/…` | text | a single navigation URL (§9) | 40 | `autoprove/…` | JSON | `{redirect}`; **text** with HTTP status 0 on timeout | 41 | `source`, `message` | text | the theory source, verbatim (§9) | 42 43 `main/*` sub-handlers (observed, exhaustive over the corpus): 44 45 | sub-route | meaning | 46 |-----------|---------| 47 | `main/help` | theory header + wellformedness report + keyboard help | 48 | `main/message` | signature + construction/deconstruction rules | 49 | `main/rules` | multiset rewriting rules (+ restrictions) | 50 | `main/tactic` | tactic(s) listing | 51 | `main/cases/{raw\|refined}/{level}/{n}` | source cases | 52 | `main/lemma/{name}` | lemma summary | 53 | `main/proof/{lemma}/{path…}` | constraint system + applicable methods at a proof node; `path` is a `/`-joined sequence of case-name segments, root `_` | 54 | `main/method/{lemma}/{n}` | apply method `n` → `{redirect}` | 55 | `main/add/{pos}` | add-lemma form (`pos` = a lemma name or `` ⇒ `%3Cfirst%3E`) | 56 | `main/edit/{name}` | edit-lemma form | 57 | `main/delete/{name}` | delete-lemma confirmation | 58 59 `overview/*` mirrors `main/*` for the pages that render a full shell: 60 `overview/help`, `overview/proof/{lemma}/{path…}`. `intdot/*` and 61 `interactive-graph-def/*` take the same `proof/{lemma}/{path…}` (and 62 `cases/…`) tails. 63 64 ### Authoritative handler/kind census (all 81 manifests, 48824 dynamic routes) 65 66 Enumerated with a template pass ([Q020]–[Q021]); the response `kind` is fixed 67 per handler: 68 69 | handler | kind | count | argument shape | 70 |--------------------------|-------------|-------|----------------| 71 | `main` | json | 17609 | see `main/*` table above | 72 | `interactive-graph-def` | dot | 14705 | `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 73 | `intdot` | html | 14705 | `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 74 | `overview` | html | 473 | `help` \| `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 75 | `next` | text | 392 | `normal/proof/{lemma}` | 76 | `prev` | text | 392 | `normal/proof/{lemma}` | 77 | `autoprove` | json (text on timeout) | 392 | `idfs/{bound}/False/proof/{lemma}` | 78 | `source` | text | 81 | (none) | 79 | `message` | text | 81 | (none) | 80 81 `autoprove/{strategy}/{bound}/{allSol}/proof/{lemma}[/path…]` is the full 82 autoprove shape (see §13.2). It answers with a `{redirect}` to the resolved 83 `overview/proof/{lemma}` at a **new** version index — live probe [L6]/[L9] show 84 `autoprove/idfs/0/False/proof/types` on a version-1 theory returning 85 `{"redirect":"/thy/trace/2/overview/proof/nonce_secrecy"}`. The 6 corpus 86 "status 0" entries carry the body `REQUEST_ERROR: TimeoutError('timed out')`, 87 which is the **crawler's own** timeout string (the client gave up while the 88 prover ran), not a web-layer response family ([Q035] — corrects the earlier note). 89 90 `next`/`prev` are pure text: a single navigation URL. The target need not be a 91 proof node — live probe shows `prev/normal/proof/types` (the first lemma) 92 returning `/thy/trace/1/main/cases/refined/0/0`. 93 94 Content-types (live probe [L6]): json = `application/json; charset=utf-8`, 95 text = `text/plain; charset=utf-8`, html = `text/html; charset=utf-8`. 96 97 Static/other routes seen in emitted links (not in the crawl of dynamic content): 98 `GET /` (index), `POST /` (upload), `POST /thy/…/reload`, 99 `GET /thy/…/download/{file}`, `POST /thy/…/get_and_append/{file}`, 100 `GET /thy/…/edit/{edit|add|delete}/{name}` (form POST targets), 101 `/static/**`, `/static/LICENSE`, `/static/img/tamarin-logo-3-0-0.png`. 102 103 Unmatched paths → **404** full HTML page (§7). 104 105 --- 106 107 ## 2. Response envelopes 108 109 ### JSON (`main/*`, `method/*`, `autoprove/*`) 110 Exactly two compact shapes, keys in this order, **no** insignificant whitespace, 111 **no** trailing newline: 112 113 ``` 114 {"html":,"title":} — content pane 115 {"redirect":} — client should navigate to the URL 116 ``` 117 118 JSON string escaping is standard: only `"` `\` and control chars are escaped; 119 `/ < > &` are **not** escaped; non-ASCII is emitted as **literal UTF-8** (no 120 `\uXXXX`). `serde_json`'s default output reproduces this byte-for-byte 121 (verified on 2450 distinct bodies). 122 123 ### HTML (`overview/*`, `intdot/*`, `/`, 404) 124 `text/html; charset=utf-8`. Full documents beginning `\n…` 125 and ending `` with **no** trailing newline. 126 127 ### text (`source`, `message`, `next`, `prev`) 128 `text/plain`. Returned verbatim; `source`/`message` end at the theory's final 129 `end` with no trailing newline; `next`/`prev` are a bare URL path. 130 131 ### DOT (`interactive-graph-def/*`) 132 Graphviz source; the empty-graph skeleton ends with `}\n`. 133 134 --- 135 136 ## 3. Escaping rules 137 138 The web layer's HTML text escape maps five characters and passes everything else 139 (including all prover-emitted unicode operators) through unchanged: 140 141 ``` 142 & -> & 143 < -> < 144 > -> > 145 " -> " 146 ' -> ' 147 ``` 148 149 Applied to: the "loaded from" path, lemma names/text in the edit form, the add 150 form's position marker (`` → `<first>` in the form action), the 151 echoed path in the 404 page. Each mapping was witnessed in a capture. Prover 152 fragments arrive already escaped (e.g. pair terms show `< … >`), so the 153 web layer does not double-escape them. 154 155 URL segments are percent-encoded independently: `` appears as 156 `%3Cfirst%3E` in hrefs. 157 158 --- 159 160 ## 4. What is web-layer vs prover-produced 161 162 Web layer (reproduced by `web-clean`): route dispatch; the JSON envelopes; the 163 page shell / index / 404 / intdot templates; the proof-script pane scaffolding 164 (theory header link, item links, add/edit/delete links, `by sorry`, blank-line 165 spacing, `end`); the proof-tree **line** markup (indentation, anchors, 166 `case`/`next`/`qed`/`by` keywords); the form bodies; HTML escaping. 167 168 Prover fragments (supplied as opaque inputs): pretty-printed signatures, rules, 169 lemma formulas, constraint systems, applicable-method names, source cases, 170 proof-method texts, non-empty DOT graphs, the theory source string, wf-warning 171 text, the rules label/count and sources descriptions. 172 173 --- 174 175 ## 5. 1	//! Route model for the interactive web UI.
2	//!
3	//! Every observed request path has the shape
4	//! `/thy/<theory-kind>/<index>/<handler>/<args…>` where:
5	//! * `<theory-kind>` is the analysis kind — only `trace` appears in the corpus
6	//!   (a `diff` kind is plausible but unobserved).
7	//! * `<index>` is either `#` (the "current" theory version) or a decimal number.
8	//! * `<handler>` selects the response family. Observed handlers and their
9	//!   response `kind`:
10	//!     - `main/…`                    -> JSON envelope (`{html,title}` / `{redirect}`)
11	//!     - `overview/…`                -> full-page HTML
12	//!     - `intdot/…`                  -> HTML mini-page
13	//!     - `interactive-graph-def/…`   -> DOT
14	//!     - `next/…`, `prev/…`          -> text (a navigation URL)
15	//!     - `autoprove/…`               -> JSON (`{redirect}`), or text on timeout
16	//!     - `source`, `message`         -> text (theory source)
17	//!
18	//! Under `main`, the sub-handlers are: `help`, `message`, `rules`, `tactic`,
19	//! `cases/{raw|refined}/{level}/{n}`, `lemma/{name}`, `add/{pos}`,
20	//! `edit/{name}`, `delete/{name}`, `method/{lemma}/{n}`, and
21	//! `proof/{lemma}/{path…}` (the proof path is a sequence of case-name segments,
22	//! the root being `_`).
23	//!
24	//! This module parses a path into a structured value; it is descriptive (the
25	//! route grammar as observed), not a dispatcher.
26	
27	/// Theory version selector.
28	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
29	pub enum Index {
30	    /// `#` — the server's "current" theory version.
31	    Current,
32	    /// An explicit decimal version index.
33	    Num(u64),
34	}
35	
36	impl Index {
37	    fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<Index> {
38	        if s == "#" {
39	            Some(Index::Current)
40	        } else {
41	            s.parse::<u64>().ok().map(Index::Num)
42	        }
43	    }
44	}

46	/// A `main/*` sub-handler.
47	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
48	pub enum Main {
49	    Help,
50	    Message,
51	    Rules,
52	    Tactic,
53	    Cases { refined: bool, level: usize, n: usize },
54	    Lemma(String),
55	    Add(String),
56	    Edit(String),
57	    Delete(String),
58	    /// Apply proof method `n` at the node identified by `path` (a `/`-joined
59	    /// sequence of case-name segments, root `_`). The method number precedes the
60	    /// path in the URL: `method/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]` (observed live [L8] and in
61	    /// 67988 body links [Q032]).
62	    Method { lemma: String, n: usize, path: Vec<String> },
63	    Proof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
64	    /// Observational-equivalence proof view (`main/diffProof/{lemma}[/path…]`),
65	    /// the diff-mode analogue of `Proof`.
66	    DiffProof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
67	    /// Apply a diff proof method (`main/diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]`), the
68	    /// diff-mode analogue of `Method`.
69	    DiffMethod { lemma: String, n: usize, path: Vec<String> },
70	    /// The diff construction/deconstruction rules view (`main/diffrules`).
71	    DiffRules,
72	    /// Any unrecognized `main/*` tail.
73	    Other(Vec<String>),
74	}

76	/// The selected handler and its arguments.
77	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
78	pub enum Handler {
79	    Source,
80	    Message,
81	    Main(Main),
82	    Overview(Vec<String>),
83	    Intdot(Vec<String>),
84	    InteractiveGraphDef(Vec<String>),
85	    Next(Vec<String>),
86	    Prev(Vec<String>),
87	    Autoprove(Vec<String>),
88	    /// Autoprove a single diff lemma (`autoproveDiff/{strategy}/{bound}/diffProof/…`).
89	    AutoproveDiff(Vec<String>),
90	    /// Autoprove all lemmas (`autoproveAll/{strategy}/{bound}`).
91	    AutoproveAll(Vec<String>),
92	    /// Re-read the theory from disk in place (`reload`; POST).
93	    Reload,
94	    /// Download the theory source (`download/{file}`; GET). The filename is a
95	    /// decorative URL segment — the body is always the current theory's source.
96	    Download(String),
97	    /// Append modified lemmas to the on-disk file (`get_and_append/{file}`; POST).
98	    GetAndAppend(String),
99	    /// A structural-edit form target (`edit/{verb}/{name}`; POST). The tail is the
100	    /// `{verb}/{name}` remainder.
101	    Edit(Vec<String>),
102	    /// Delete a theory path (`del/path/{theory-path…}`; GET). The tail is the
103	    /// theory-path segments after the fixed `path` literal. It is parsed into a
104	    /// [`ThyPath`] by the dispatcher (mode-aware): an unparseable tail is a `404`,
105	    /// a parseable one with a non-GET method is a `405`.
106	    DelPath(Vec<String>),
107	    /// Verify a theory path (`verify/{theory-path…}`; GET; trace theories only).
108	    /// The tail is the theory-path segments, parsed into a [`ThyPath`] by the
109	    /// dispatcher.
110	    Verify(Vec<String>),
111	    /// Unrecognized handler with its raw tail.
112	    Other { name: String, tail: Vec<String> },
113	}

115	/// A parsed route.
116	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
117	pub struct Route {
118	    pub theory_kind: String,
119	    pub index: Index,
120	    pub handler: Handler,
121	}
122	
123	fn owned(v: &[&str]) -> Vec<String> {
124	    v.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect()
125	}
126	
127	fn parse_main(tail: &[&str]) -> Main {
128	    match tail {
129	        ["help"] => Main::Help,
130	        ["message"] => Main::Message,
131	        ["rules"] => Main::Rules,
132	        ["diffrules"] => Main::DiffRules,
133	        ["tactic"] => Main::Tactic,
134	        ["cases", kind, level, n]
135	            if (*kind == "raw" || *kind == "refined")
136	                && level.parse::<usize>().is_ok()
137	                && n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() =>
138	        {
139	            Main::Cases {
140	                refined: *kind == "refined",
141	                level: level.parse().unwrap(),
142	                n: n.parse().unwrap(),
143	            }
144	        }
145	        ["lemma", name] => Main::Lemma((*name).to_string()),
146	        ["add", pos] => Main::Add((*pos).to_string()),
147	        ["edit", name] => Main::Edit((*name).to_string()),
148	        ["delete", name] => Main::Delete((*name).to_string()),
149	        ["method", lemma, n, rest @ ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Main::Method {
150	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
151	            n: n.parse().unwrap(),
152	            path: owned(rest),
153	        },
154	        ["diffMethod", lemma, n, rest @ ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Main::DiffMethod {
155	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
156	            n: n.parse().unwrap(),
157	            path: owned(rest),
158	        },
159	        ["diffProof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Main::DiffProof {
160	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
161	            path: owned(rest),
162	        },
163	        [proof, lemma, rest @ ..] if *proof == "proof" => Main::Proof {
164	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
165	            path: owned(rest),
166	        },
167	        _ => Main::Other(owned(tail)),
168	    }
169	}
170	
171	impl Route {
172	    /// Parse a request path such as `/thy/trace/#/main/proof/exec/_/B_2`.
173	    /// Returns `None` if the path is not under `/thy/<kind>/<index>/…`.
174	    pub fn parse(path: &str) -> Option<Route> {
175	        let trimmed = path.strip_prefix('/').unwrap_or(path);
176	        let segs: Vec<&str> = trimmed.split('/').collect();
177	        // Need at least: thy / kind / index / handler
178	        if segs.len() < 4 || segs[0] != "thy" {
179	            return None;
180	        }
181	        let theory_kind = segs[1].to_string();
182	        let index = Index::parse(segs[2])?;
183	        let handler_name = segs[3];
184	        let tail = &segs[4..];
185	        let handler = match handler_name {
186	            "source" => Handler::Source,
187	            "message" => Handler::Message,
188	            "main" => Handler::Main(parse_main(tail)),
189	            "overview" => Handler::Overview(owned(tail)),
190	            "intdot" => Handler::Intdot(owned(tail)),
191	            "interactive-graph-def" => Handler::InteractiveGraphDef(owned(tail)),
192	            "next" => Handler::Next(owned(tail)),
193	            "prev" => Handler::Prev(owned(tail)),
194	            "autoprove" => Handler::Autoprove(owned(tail)),
195	            "autoproveDiff" => Handler::AutoproveDiff(owned(tail)),
196	            "autoproveAll" => Handler::AutoproveAll(owned(tail)),
197	            "reload" => Handler::Reload,
198	            "download" => match tail {
199	                [file] => Handler::Download((*file).to_string()),
200	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "download".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
201	            },
202	            "get_and_append" => match tail {
203	                [file] => Handler::GetAndAppend((*file).to_string()),
204	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "get_and_append".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
205	            },
206	            "edit" => Handler::Edit(owned(tail)),
207	            // `del/path/<theory-path…>` — the fixed `path` literal must follow.
208	            "del" => match tail {
209	                ["path", rest @ ..] => Handler::DelPath(owned(rest)),
210	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "del".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
211	            },
212	            "verify" => Handler::Verify(owned(tail)),
213	            other => Handler::Other {
214	                name: other.to_string(),
215	                tail: owned(tail),
216	            },
217	        };
218	        Some(Route {
219	            theory_kind,
220	            index,
221	            handler,
222	        })
223	    }
224	}

226	/// The full request surface: the top-level (non-`/thy`) routes plus the
227	/// theory-scoped `/thy/<kind>/<index>/…` routes.
228	///
229	/// `Toplevel::parse` takes the URL **path** only (any `?query` must be split off
230	/// by the caller and passed separately to the dispatcher).
231	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
232	pub enum Toplevel {
233	    /// `/` — the index page (GET) / theory upload (POST).
234	    Root,
235	    /// `/robots.txt`.
236	    Robots,
237	    /// `/favicon.ico` — a `303` redirect to the static icon.
238	    Favicon,
239	    /// `/kill` — cancel a running proof search (requires a `path` query arg).
240	    Kill,
241	    /// `/static/<path…>` — a filesystem-backed asset.
242	    Static(Vec<String>),
243	    /// A theory-scoped route.
244	    Thy(Route),
245	    /// Anything else (→ 404).
246	    Other(String),
247	}
248	
249	impl Toplevel {
250	    pub fn parse(path: &str) -> Toplevel {
251	        let trimmed = path.strip_prefix('/').unwrap_or(path);
252	        if trimmed.is_empty() {
253	            return Toplevel::Root;
254	        }
255	        let segs: Vec<&str> = trimmed.split('/').collect();
256	        match segs[0] {
257	            "robots.txt" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Robots,
258	            "favicon.ico" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Favicon,
259	            "kill" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Kill,
260	            "static" => Toplevel::Static(owned(&segs[1..])),
261	            "thy" => match Route::parse(path) {
262	                Some(r) => Toplevel::Thy(r),
263	                None => Toplevel::Other(path.to_string()),
264	            },
265	            _ => Toplevel::Other(path.to_string()),
266	        }
267	    }
268	}

270	/// A parsed `autoprove/{strategy}/{bound}/{allSol}/proof/{lemma}[/path…]` request.
271	///
272	/// Observed variant matrix (corpus [Q030], keyboard help [Q031]): `strategy` is
273	/// `idfs` (solve/prove) or `characterize` (characterization, e.g. exists-trace);
274	/// `bound` is a depth bound (`0` = unbounded — the `a`/`A`/`s`/`S` shortcuts; `5`
275	/// = bounded — the `b`/`B` shortcuts); `all_solutions` is the `True`/`False` flag
276	/// (`False` = stop after the first solution, the lowercase shortcuts; `True` =
277	/// search for all solutions, the uppercase shortcuts).
278	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
279	pub struct Autoprove {
280	    pub strategy: String,
281	    pub bound: u64,
282	    pub all_solutions: bool,
283	    pub lemma: String,
284	    pub path: Vec<String>,
285	}
286	
287	impl Autoprove {
288	    /// Parse the tail of an `autoprove/…` route (everything after `autoprove/`).
289	    pub fn parse(tail: &[String]) -> Option<Autoprove> {
290	        match tail {
291	            [strategy, bound, flag, proof, lemma, rest @ ..] if proof == "proof" => {
292	                Some(Autoprove {
293	                    strategy: strategy.clone(),
294	                    bound: bound.parse().ok()?,
295	                    all_solutions: parse_bool(flag)?,
296	                    lemma: lemma.clone(),
297	                    path: rest.to_vec(),
298	                })
299	            }
300	            _ => None,
301	        }
302	    }
303	}

441	/// A theory navigation path, as accepted by the `del/path/…` and `verify/…`
442	/// routes. The accepted heads are **mode-dependent**: a `trace` theory accepts
443	/// `help` · `message` · `rules` · `tactic` · `cases/{raw|refined}/{level}/{n}` ·
444	/// `lemma/{name}` · `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` · `method/{lemma}/{n}[/seg…]` ·
445	/// `add/{pos}` · `edit/{name}` · `delete/{name}`; an `equiv` (diff) theory accepts
446	/// `help` · `diffrules` · `diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` · `diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/…]`.
447	/// A tail outside the grammar does not parse — the route then answers `404`.
448	///
449	/// Only the lemma and proof nodes carry data the dispatcher needs; every other
450	/// accepted head collapses to [`ThyPath::Other`].
451	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
452	pub enum ThyPath {
453	    /// `lemma/{name}` (trace).
454	    Lemma(String),
455	    /// `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` (trace).
456	    Proof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
457	    /// `diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` (equiv).
458	    DiffProof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
459	    /// Any other accepted theory path (a navigable view that is neither a lemma
460	    /// nor a proof node).
461	    Other,
462	}
463	
464	impl ThyPath {
465	    /// Parse a theory-path tail. `diff` selects the equiv (diff-theory) grammar.
466	    /// Returns `None` for a tail outside the grammar (the caller then answers a
467	    /// `404`).
468	    pub fn parse(segs: &[String], diff: bool) -> Option<ThyPath> {
469	        let s: Vec<&str> = segs.iter().map(String::as_str).collect();
470	        if diff {
471	            match s.as_slice() {
472	                ["help"] | ["diffrules"] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
473	                ["diffProof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Some(ThyPath::DiffProof {
474	                    lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
475	                    path: owned(rest),
476	                }),
477	                ["diffMethod", _lemma, n, ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Some(ThyPath::Other),
478	                _ => None,
479	            }
480	        } else {
481	            match s.as_slice() {
482	                ["help"] | ["message"] | ["rules"] | ["tactic"] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
483	                ["cases", kind, level, n]
484	                    if (*kind == "raw" || *kind == "refined")
485	                        && level.parse::<usize>().is_ok()
486	                        && n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() =>
487	                {
488	                    Some(ThyPath::Other)
489	                }
490	                ["lemma", name] => Some(ThyPath::Lemma((*name).to_string())),
491	                ["proof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Some(ThyPath::Proof {
492	                    lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
493	                    path: owned(rest),
494	                }),
495	                ["method", _lemma, n, ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Some(ThyPath::Other),
496	                ["add", _] | ["edit", _] | ["delete", _] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
497	                _ => None,
498	            }
499	        }
500	    }
501	}
502	
503	fn parse_bool(s: &str) -> Option<bool> {
504	    match s {
505	        "True" => Some(true),
506	        "False" => Some(false),
507	        _ => None,
508	    }
509	}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Live reference-server wrapper for UNIT A round 5 probing (del/path + verify).
# Usage: wl_server5.sh start <file.spthy> <port> [extra tamarin flags...]
#        wl_server5.sh stop <port>

export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
BIN="/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover"
cmd="$1"; shift
case "$cmd" in
  start)
    f="$1"; port="$2"; shift 2
    d=$(mktemp -d)

    echo "stopped $port"
    ;;
esac

---

## 17. Concurrency semantics — snapshot → compute → commit dispatch (Round 7)

Derived from live probing of the sanctioned oracle ([R70]–[R76], ports 3100/3101,
Tutorial/NSLPK3/NAXOS_eCK/RYY_PFS trace + the `sapic/slow` PKCS11 theory as the long
op). No file under `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` was read. All servers stopped. This
section is the **behavioural contract** the round-7 dispatch redesign honours: the
reference server serves a long-running proof operation **without** freezing unrelated
(or related) requests, so the port must do the same.

### 17.1 The reference is fully concurrent under a long proof op ([R71])
With a ~30s `autoprove` (PKCS11 `cannot_obtain_key_ind`, measured 30.56s) **in flight**,
a burst fired ~2.5s in — every probe returned in its own small latency (0.02–0.52s), all
`200`, all long before the long op's 30.5s completion. Nothing blocked:

| concurrent request during the long op | served |
|---------------------------------------|:------:|
| GET on **other** theories (overview, source) | immediately |
| GET on the **same** theory being proved (overview/help) | immediately |
| GET on the same theory's **other** versions | immediately |
| a **second proof op** on a **different** theory (`method` → new version) | immediately, concurrently |
| a **second proof op** on the **same** theory (`autoprove` → new version) | immediately, concurrently |
| **upload** (`POST /`) | immediately |
| **reload** (`POST …/reload`), incl. the **same** base index | immediately |

So a slow `ProverOps` computation must NOT hold any exclusive lock on the server or the
state backend: reads, mutations, and other proof ops all proceed alongside it.

### 17.2 Version allocation is at COMMIT, not at request start ([R72],[R73])
One global monotonic counter (§§13.1/14.3). The fresh index for a proof operation is
allocated **when the operation completes** (commits its result), not when the request
arrives:

* In the burst, the long op **started first** (t=0.014s) but **committed last**
  (t=30.57s) and received the **highest** index (10); the two fast ops that committed at
  t=2.57/2.73s received the **lower** indices 8/9. Indices follow **completion order**,
  independent of start order ([R72]).
* The to-be-allocated version is **invisible/unresolvable during the computation**:
  index-page polling every ~1.4s across the whole 30s op never showed the new index until
  **after** completion ([R73]). A version becomes resolvable exactly at its commit.

### 17.3 Concurrent allocation never collides or skips ([R74])
12 truly-simultaneous fast proof ops allocated indices 12..23 — 12 **distinct,
contiguous** indices, **zero** collisions, **zero** skips. Allocation is atomic: each
committing op takes the next counter value under mutual exclusion, and the counter is the
only thing that must be briefly serialized.

### 17.4 Snapshot isolation ([R75])
A long `autoprove` on base index 3 completed successfully (→ index 24) **even though that
base was `reload`-ed in place at t=3–8s during the computation** — the in-place reload was
itself served concurrently (redirect to idx3/help) and neither corrupted nor aborted the
long op. The long computation operates on the **base snapshot it read at the start**; a
concurrent in-place replace of that base does not affect the in-flight result. Retention
reconfirmed: idx 4 dropped from the capped index-page window but still resolved (`200`).

### 17.5 The dispatch contract this implies (implemented in Round 7)
Every request is processed as **get-snapshot → compute → commit**:

1. **get-snapshot.** Resolve the requested index and take a cheap **owned snapshot** of
   that version through `StateOps::snapshot` (releasing any backend lock immediately). No
   state borrow is held past this step.
2. **compute.** Run the `ProverOps` call — including the possibly-slow ones (`autoprove*`,
   `apply_method`/`apply_diff_method`, `del_*`, `reload`, `load_theory`, edits) — on the
   snapshot **with no state lock held**. Concurrent requests take their own snapshots and
   run in parallel (§17.1/§17.4).
3. **commit.** Apply the result with a **separate, atomic** `StateOps` call:
   `insert_new` for a proof op / upload / `del/path` (allocates the fresh monotonic index
   **now**, at commit — §17.2/§17.3), or `replace` for an in-place `reload`/structural
   edit. Reads commit nothing.

`Server::dispatch` therefore takes **`&self`** (not `&mut self`): the server is shared
across concurrent requests, and all mutation lives behind the `StateOps` backend's
interior mutability. `StateOps` is now an **interior-mutability, snapshot-handing** trait
(`&self` everywhere; `snapshot`/`entries` return owned handles; `insert_new`/`replace`/
`remove` mutate atomically). The reference `InMemoryState<T>` provides this with a
`Mutex` around the `BTreeMap`+counter; a consumer's async cache implements the same
`&self` façade. The observed lifecycle — monotonic **commit-time** allocation, atomicity
under races, retention, in-place mutation, snapshot isolation — is the documented
contract a backend must satisfy.

### 17.6 Incidental discovery (out of round-7 scope) ([R76])
`autoprove` can **fail** with a JSON `{"alert":"Sorry, but the autoprover () failed!"}`
(observed on NAXOS `eCK_same_key`, `idfs/0/True`), analogous to the `method`-failure
alert. The current dispatcher models `autoprove` as always-redirect; modelling the alert
branch is a documented gap, deferred to keep the round-7 change scoped to concurrency.
EOF

## Round 7 — concurrency-safe dispatch (snapshot → compute → commit)

Adoption blocker: the round-6 `Server::dispatch(&mut self)` required exclusive
ownership per request, so behind one lock a long `autoprove` (seconds-to-minutes)
would freeze the whole server. Black-box only: live probing ([R70]–[R76], ports
3100/3101, Tutorial/NSLPK3/NAXOS_eCK/RYY_PFS trace + the `sapic/slow` PKCS11 theory
as the long op) plus three committed probe scripts (`scratch/r7/*.sh`). No file under
`/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` was read. All live servers stopped.

### STEP 1 — the reference's concurrency contract (probed; BEHAVIOR §17)

Using PKCS11 `cannot_obtain_key_ind` (~30s, terminating) as an in-flight long op:

* **Fully non-blocking ([R71]).** A burst fired ~2.5s into the 30s autoprove — reads
  on other theories, reads on the SAME theory being proved, reads of other versions, a
  second proof op on a different theory, a second proof op on the SAME theory, an
  upload (`POST /`), and a reload (incl. the same base index) — every one returned in
  0.02–0.52s, all `200`, all long before the long op finished. Nothing blocked.
* **Commit-time allocation ([R72]).** The long op STARTED first (t=0.014s) but
  COMMITTED last (t=30.57s) and got the HIGHEST index (10); the two fast ops that
  committed at t≈2.6s got the lower indices 8/9. Indices follow **completion** order.
* **Invisible until commit ([R73]).** Index-page polling across the whole 30s never
  showed the long op's index until after it completed — the to-be-allocated version is
  unresolvable during the computation.
* **Atomic under races ([R74]).** 12 truly-simultaneous fast proof ops allocated
  indices 12..23 — distinct, contiguous, zero collisions, zero skips.
* **Snapshot isolation ([R75]).** A long autoprove on idx3 completed fine (→ idx24)
  even though idx3 was reloaded in place mid-computation; the reload was itself served
  concurrently. Retention reconfirmed (a capped-out index still resolves `200`).
* Incidental ([R76]): autoprove can fail with `{"alert":"Sorry, but the autoprover ()
  failed!"}` — documented gap, not addressed this round (concurrency-scoped change).

### STEP 2 — the redesign

`Server::dispatch` now takes **`&self`** (one server shared across concurrent
requests). Each request runs **get-snapshot → compute → commit**: resolve + take a
cheap owned snapshot via `StateOps::snapshot` (lock released), run the `ProverOps`
call — including the slow ones — on the snapshot with **no state lock held**, then
commit atomically (`insert_new` allocates the fresh monotonic index **at commit**;
`replace` for in-place reload/edits). The only serialized sections are the
microsecond-scale atomic `StateOps` calls. Design note: I kept a single `dispatch`
entry point (not a static cheap/slow route split) because the snapshot→compute→commit
pipeline already isolates the lock-free compute from the two tiny atomic state calls,
and even a "read" route may invoke a non-trivial fragment producer — classifying
routes as cheap/slow would be a lossy approximation of "hold no lock during any
`ProverOps` call".

`StateOps` was reshaped to an interior-mutability, snapshot-handing trait (all `&self`;
`get`→`snapshot` returns an owned copy; `entries`→`indices`+`snapshot`). The reference
`InMemoryState<T: Clone>` is now a `Mutex<BTreeMap + counter>`; each method holds the
lock only for the map/counter op, never across compute. `Server`/`InMemoryState`
therefore compose to `Send + Sync`, so a consumer wraps the server in an `Arc` and
serves requests from any async task; a real backend implements the same `&self` façade
over its own async cache. The probed lifecycle (commit-time monotonic allocation,
atomicity, retention, in-place mutation, snapshot isolation) is the documented contract.

### Deliverables

- `src/dispatch.rs` — `dispatch(&self)`; `StateOps` reshaped (`snapshot`/`indices`,
  all `&self`); `InMemoryState` behind a `Mutex`; every handler restructured to take
  the request's single snapshot and commit via a separate atomic `StateOps` call.
- `tests/dispatch7.rs` (2 tests) — a `GatedProver` whose `autoprove` parks mid-compute
  on a controllable gate: `slow_op_in_flight_is_non_blocking_and_commits_last` asserts
  the full probed interleaving (non-blocking read while parked; a fast op commits first
  → index 2; the parked op's index invisible/unresolvable pre-commit; on release it
  commits last → index 3; final set contiguous), and `concurrent_allocations_never_
  collide_or_skip` races 16 threads and asserts a contiguous unique index block ([R74]).
- `tests/dispatch6.rs` — `WrapState` custom backend + `InMemoryState` contract tests
  updated to the `&self` snapshot API. All round-3/4/5/6 tests unchanged in intent
  (`let mut s` → `let s`; `dispatch5`'s `body` helper takes `&Server`).
- BEHAVIOR.md §17 (+ §16.2 superseding note); QUERIES.log [R70]–[R76].

`cargo test` → **104 passing** (26 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 14 dispatch5 +
7 dispatch6 + **2 dispatch7** + 23 parity); `cargo clippy --tests --examples` clean;
`dispatch7` verified non-flaky over 20 consecutive runs. All prior byte-parity tests
stay green (the state reshape is behind `Server::new`/`InMemoryState`, unchanged output).
EOF
